sections.markdown:
# CL-WHO: HTML Generation CL-WHO turns S-expressions into HTML. The mapping is direct: a list `(TAG . CONTENTS)` becomes `...`. Nested lists nest tags. Strings appear as text content. ```lisp (cl-who:with-html-output-to-string (s) (:html (:head (:title "hi")) (:body (:h1 "Hello") (:p "world")))) ``` => `hi

Hello

world

` The macro provides three local helpers inside its body: * `(cl-who:str EXPR)` — evaluate `EXPR`, write the result as a string. * `(cl-who:fmt CONTROL &rest ARGS)` — `format` writing to the output stream. * `(cl-who:esc EXPR)` — like `str`, but HTML-escapes `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`. * `(cl-who:htm TAG ...)` — escape from a runtime context back into the macro's tag-DSL. Attributes are written as a flat list right after the tag keyword: `(:a :href "/x" "link text")` => `link text`. CL-WHO has no concept of components or templates — it's the bare-metal generator. Pair it with your own helper functions for reuse.

sections.explain: A single tag with text content.
Attributes are interleaved keyword/value pairs after the tag.
STR writes a runtime value into the output. HTM lets us return to the tag DSL after the LET.

sections.prompt:
Define RENDER-TITLE (S) returning an HTML string of the form "

S

" (no escaping, S is plain ASCII without special chars). Use CL-WHO:WITH-HTML-OUTPUT-TO-STRING and CL-WHO:STR.
